title: ceropegia modestantha
intro: Ceropegia modestantha, also known as the 'Coral Cactus', is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to India and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and improving respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Ceropegia modestantha are typically used to make infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be administered to treat a range of ailments.
